one_time_description: Share of income from Alexis owned enterprises (government business enterprises accounted for by modified equity method)
economic_notes: Nation has government business enterprises that encountered difficulties completing contracted services due to COVID-19. Nation operates Nakota Crossing. COVID-19 impacted ability to generate self-source revenues through subsidiaries.
notes: Audit opinion is QUALIFIED due to inability to obtain sufficient appropriate audit evidence about carrying amount of investments in government business enterprises and advances to Alexis owned enterprises. Prior period restatement made in 2020 to correct incorrectly recorded advances from Alexis owned enterprises, reducing 2019 accumulated opening surplus by $5,670,302 and advances by $8,721,852. Two consolidated subsidiaries: Northern ISGA Foundation and Alexis Board of Education. GBEs accounted for by modified equity method. COVID-19 noted as subsequent event risk. Capital projects expenses of $3,717,582 and Northern ISGA Foundation expenses of $1,216,437 also reported. ISC funding calculated as Block + Fixed + Flexible + Set funding = $14,914,164; total government transfers includes ISC + Health Canada + Government of Alberta + Northern ISGA Foundation + CMHC + Yellowhead Tribal + Consultation revenue approximated. OSR estimated as Other revenue + Alexis Settlement Trust + net carry-forwards.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
